subject: GRB 260112B: GECAM-B observation
body:
Hao-Xuan Guo, Chen-Wei Wang, Shao-Lin Xiong (IHEP) report on behalf of GECAM team:

GECAM-B was triggered on-ground by GRB 260112B, at 2026-01-12T20:45:55.000 UTC (denoted as T0), which is also detected by Fermi/GBM (Fermi GBM team, GCN #43380).

According to the GECAM-B light curves in about 70-6000 keV, this burst mainly consists of multiple pulses with a duration (T90) of 15.9 +1.7/-2.2 s.

The GECAM-B light curve can be found here:
https://www.bursthub.cn//admin/static/gecambgrb260112B.png

Gravitational wave high-energy Electromagnetic Counterpart All-sky Monitor (GECAM) mission originally consists of two micro-satellites (GECAM-A and GECAM-B) launched in Dec. 2020. As the third member of GECAM constellation, GECAM-C was launched onboard SATech-01 experimental satellite in July 2022. GECAM mission is funded by the Chinese Academy of Sciences (CAS).
